Home / Privacy Policy

Privacy Policy

How the site uses analytics, advertising, and limited technical data.

This Privacy Policy explains how Beat the Scam uses analytics, advertising, and standard website technologies when you browse the site.

What information we collect

The site does not offer user accounts, comments, or direct purchases. Standard server logs may record technical information such as browser type, device type, approximate location, referral source, and requested pages.

Google Analytics

The site uses Google Analytics 4 to understand page performance and traffic trends. The configured measurement ID is G-JXNF856NBF. Analytics cookies are only enabled after consent where required.

Advertising

The site uses Google AdSense with publisher ID ca-pub-1606633100797174. If advertising is active, Google and its partners may use cookies or similar technologies to deliver and measure ads, subject to your consent choices and applicable law.

Cookie choices

A cookie banner allows you to accept or reject non-essential cookies. Your preference is stored locally in your browser so the site can remember that choice.

Your rights

If you are in the UK or EEA, you may have rights relating to access, correction, or deletion of personal data depending on the context. Because the site is intentionally low-data, many visits involve minimal personal data processing.

Contact

For privacy-related queries, email hello@beatthescam.com.

Beat the Scam

Scam alerts, plain-English checks, and practical guides to help people verify suspicious emails, texts, websites, calls, and offers.

Educational content only. Not legal, regulatory, or financial advice.

Cookies & privacy

We use analytics and advertising cookies to understand performance and support the site. Choose whether to allow non-essential cookies.
